title: verbascum × subcaudatum
intro: Verbascum × subcaudatum, also known as the hybrid Mullein, is a cross between Verbascum blattaria and Verbascum lychnitis, two species of flowering plants in the mullein genus. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of rosmarinic acid and saponins. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as promoting wound healing and soothing skin irritations. The herbal preparations of Verbascum × subcaudatum include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
